category_title: Students who repeat the teacher's text to avoid reading new material
scenario_title: Shared reading avoidance through text repetition
minimal_context: The student and interventionist are taking turns reading 'Miss Nelson is missing!' during a one-on-one session.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: I will read this line: 'The kids were being rude or silly.' Now it is your turn to read.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: That's the part I read. Try the next line.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I just said... was rude or silly.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: You's gonna read the next one. I read this one.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: I will read this line: 'The kids were being rude or silly.' Now it is your turn to read.

STUDENT: Was rude or that one.

TUTOR: That's the part I read. Try the next line.

STUDENT: I just said... was rude or silly.

TUTOR: What's the next one say?

STUDENT: You's gonna read the next one. I read this one.

task.content: Shared reading of Miss Nelson is missing! (Allard and Marshall, 1977)

context.social_setting: One-on-one shared reading session with an interventionist
context.time_context: The fifth, sixth and seventh sessions

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When the interventionist finishes reading a passage and signals for the student to take a turn via a gaze shift
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Declines to read. Instead he repeats exactly what the adult had previously read, debates what line to read, and counters by telling the interventionist to read the next one.
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: Recognizing that an interactive turn is required on his part due to the previously established pedagogical expectations... delays having to read or potentially read unsuccessfully
related_quotes: I just said (unintelligible). Was rude or silly.
You ’s gonna read the next one. I read this one.

behavior_patterns.condition: Rather than accepting the invitation and attempting the next portion of unread text
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Accepted a speaking turn and repeated exactly what the adult had previously read

behavior_patterns.condition: When the interventionist clarifies the reading invitation and points to the next line
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Debates what line to read, persists in his avoidance, and counters by telling the interventionist to read
behavior_patterns.internal_state: Shifting the intervention ’s attention away from the text
related_quotes: I just said (unintelligible). Was rude or silly.
You ’s gonna read the next one. I read this one.
